observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Municipal Treasurer fast-track the updating of the assessment roll through a frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical and manpower support are provided to that Office.
observations.reason: Ongoing implementation of the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly implement the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance to secure delinquent rentals and surcharges. Also, consider other proactive strategies to enjoin the stall holders to pay their delinquencies to sustain the local revenue sources beneficial to the LGU.
observations.reason: Ongoing collection of the unpaid rental fees and surcharges

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate the expropriation proceedings by filing a verified complaint before the proper court against the property owner of the site location for the development projects to promote the general welfare of the people.
observations.reason: Ongoing appraisal valuation is still with DBP. Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to use the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax due and demandable; instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to reconcile the variance noted on the Assessed Value of Real Properties; and to update the RPT/SET receivables so that the total amount of uncollected real property tax can be properly identified.
observations.reason: Ongoing reconciliation of the records

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to: a. direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to take necessary steps to strengthen the tax collection strategies of the agency; b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish a precise monitoring of delinquent tax payers; c. direct the Assessor to recommend to the sanggunian the strategies relating to tax ordinance and revenue measures for the collection of RPT; and d. direct the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality to support the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor in strengthening the agency’s collection strategies.
observations.reason: Ongoing reconciliation of the records

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the
affected accounts in the financial statements;
b. Submit to the audit team the supporting documents of the recorded
Accounts Payable; and
c. Verify the validity of the recorded claims in the prior years of the Accounts
Payable and undertake the appropriate action as necessary.

2.8 We recommended that the LCE direct:
a. the GSO, together with the inventory committee, to conduct the physical
count of inventories accurately and completely in accordance with the
above-mentioned provisions to fairly present the Inventory account in the
financial statements;
b. the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to conduct a periodic reconciliation
of the Inventory account to ensure that the balance of the said account on
their separate records is reconciled at year end.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer reconcile their records to reflect the correct amount of utilization of the
LDRRMF for the period and make the necessary journal entry/ies in the books
of accounts as necessary.

4.7 We recommended that the LCE ensure that PPAs planned to support disaster
risk reduction management are realized and implemented on time so that the
public can enjoy the benefits.

5.11 We recommended that the LCE ensure that those identified development
projects are realized and implemented on time to afford the public the benefits
that can be derived therefrom.

6.9 We recommended that the:
a. JO personnel to submit the authenticated copies of logbook entries as
additional supporting documents for the payment of their wages and ensure
that time entries on DTRs reconcile with the records of attendance as
reflected on the logbook; and
b. LCE/HRMO provide a written justification for the 130 Job Order
personnel who were absent during the headcount along with a valid
document and identification proving their existence.

7.8 We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that
the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report
are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period as required in
Paragraph V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, together with the required
supporting documents.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant comply with the required reconciliation to sort out the unreconciled differences between their respective records and ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE account.

We recommended that the LCE direct: a) the GSO to conduct the physical count of inventories accurately and completely in accordance with the above-mentioned provisions to fairly present the Inventory account in the financial statements; and b) the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to conduct a periodic reconciliation of the Inventory account to ensure that the balance of the said account on their separate records is reconciled at year end.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer return to the Trust Fund the amount of ₱390,573.38 which was erroneously transferred to the General Fund and for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries accordingly.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Accumulated Depreciation account to be able to present the PPE valuation.

We recommended that management judiciously undertake the post-qualification process in accordance with Section 34 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that contracts are awarded only to bidders who pass the criteria for post-qualification and thus be considered as the bidder with the lowest calculated and responsive bid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the reporting requirement of the LGU for the implementation of the programs to be funded by LGSF-SBDP as outlined in Item 5.2 of the Local Budget Circular No. 144 is faithfully adhered by the personnel concerned.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the MDRRM Officer to include all the unexpended prior year balance of LDRRMF in the preparation of its LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the auditor on or before the 15th day following the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF are approved in order to allocate the funds to their intended use and provide the community with the benefits that the fund envisions.

We recommended that the: a) Municipal Assessor fast-track the updating of the assessment roll through a frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical and manpower support are provided to that Office. Municipal Treasurer strategizes and strengthen collection drive beyond the required posting and sending out of notices to support and sustain the LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its constituents.
observations.reason: Ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly implement the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance to secure delinquent municipal rentals and surcharges. Also, consider other proactive strategies to enjoin the stall holders to pay their delinquencies to sustain the local revenue sources beneficial to the LGU.
observations.reason: Ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consult the Provincial Legal Officer on the initiation of the expropriation proceedings by filing a verified complaint before the proper court against the property owner of the site location for the development projects to promote the general welfare of the people.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to use the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax due and demandable; instruct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the variance noted on Assessed Value of Real Properties; and instruct the Municipal Assessor to reconcile the records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the records is still ongoing with DBP. Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials see to it that the 20% Development Fund be fully utilized and implemented immediately as embodied in the Annual Procurement Plan, in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to take necessary steps to strengthen the tax collection strategies of the agency; direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish a precise monitoring of delinquent tax payers; direct the Municipal Assessor to recommend to the sanggunian the strategies relating to tax ordinance and revenue measures for the collection of RPT; and direct the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality to support the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor in strengthening the agency’s collection strategies.
observations.reason: Ongoing

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal
Accountant comply with the required periodic reconciliation to sort out the
unreconciled differences between their respective records and ascertain the
accuracy and existence of the PPE account.

2.8 We recommended that the LCE direct:
a) the GSO to conduct the physical count of inventories accurately and
completely in accordance with the above-mentioned provisions to fairly
present the Inventory account in the financial statements; and
b) the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to conduct a periodic reconciliation
of the Inventory account to ensure that the balance of the said account on
their separate records is reconciled at yearend.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer return to the Trust Fund the
amount of ₱390,573.38 which was erroneously transferred to the General Fund
and for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries
accordingly.

4.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the
understatement of the Accumulated Depreciation account to be able to present
the PPE in its proper valuation.

5.17 We recommended that management judiciously undertake the post-
qualification process in accordance with Section 34 of the 2016 Revised IRR of
RA 9184 to ensure that contracts are awarded only to bidders who pass the
criteria for post-qualification and thus be considered as the bidder with the
lowest calculated and responsive bid.

6.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the reporting
requirement of the LGU for the implementation of the programs to be funded
by LGSF-SBDP as outlined in Item 5.2 of the Local Budget Circular No. 144 is
faithfully adhered to by the personnel concerned.

7.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the development
projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-
and-implementation-ready.

8.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRM Officer
to include all the unexpended prior year balance of LDRRMF transferred to the
Trust Liability – DRRM account in the preparation of its LDRRMFIP.

9.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer to
prepare and submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the
LDRRMF to the COA auditor on or before the 15th day following the end of
each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) of
the LGU.

10.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those
allowable charges against the LDRRMF are approved in order to allocate the
funds to their intended use and provide the community with the benefits that
the fund envisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – a) Draw the necessary adjusting entry to correct the Road Network System account and ascertain the reliability of the account; and, Strictly comply with the required disclosure of the account in the notes to financial statements for the fair presentation of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entry to correct the year-end balance of the Cash-in-Bank account and strictly observe the check disbursement process and reporting to maintain the reliability of the account presentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer undertake collaborative procedures to reconcile their respective records based on available reliable source documents to ascertain the accuracy and fair presentation of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – a) Immediately draw an adjusting entry for the inclusion of the bank account with an existing balance to arrive at the correct amount for the Cash-in-Bank account; and process the closing of the bank account to recover the existing balance and avoid the gradual charges or deduction thereof as a dormant account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive rescinds the individual contract of service to consider the daily rate instead of monthly payments, to maintain equality and fairness among personnel hired under the same contract, and, secure the required authorization from the local sanggunian before entering into contract of service with private individuals; and, HRMO strictly observes the conditions set forth under the CSC Resolution No. 020790 to facilitate an appropriate term with the hired private individuals through a Job Order Contract or Contract of Services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer on any pending projects of the LGU funded by National Agencies to ascertain the amount attributable to the Due to NGAs-Other account and establish the required subsidiary ledgers to attain reliable and accurate amount of the account, thus, take opportunity on the benefits from the original projects identified from the fund.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee to formulate appropriate Programs, Projects and tax/revenue measures to support the budget.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer on any pending projects of the LGU to ascertain the amount attributable to the account, thus, take opportunity on the benefits from the original projects identified on the fund
observations.reason: Request for write-off was sent to the Regional Office but denied since only receivables outstanding were covered by cash advances attributable to the Development Officer on any pending projects of the LGU to ascertain the amount attributable to the account, thus, take opportunity on the benefits from the original projects identified on the fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately consult the Provincial Legal Officer on the initiation of the expropriation proceedings by filing a verified complaint before the proper court against the property owners of the site locations for the development projects to promote the general welfare of the people
observations.reason: On-going expropriation process

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) direct the Accounting Accountant to use the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax due and demandable submitted by the Municipal Treasurer as basis for setting up the RPT/SET receivables at the start of the year; b) instruct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the variance noted on the Assessed Value of Real Properties; and c) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax due and demandable.
observations.reason: reconciliation between the MTO and Accountant to use the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax due and demandable submitted by the Municipal Treasurer as basis for setting up the RPT/SET receivables at the start of the year; b. instruct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the variance noted on the Assessed Value of Real Properties; and c. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card, Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax due and demandable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials see to it that the 20% Development Fund be fully utilized and immediately implemented as embodied in the Annual Procurement Plan, in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.reason: DF includes irrelevant projects and most have no ROW yet

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant –
a) Draw the necessary adjusting entry to correct the Road Network System
account and ascertain the reliability of the account; and,
b) Strictly comply with the required disclosure of the account in the notes to
financial statements for its fair presentation.
1.7 The Municipal Accountant agreed to the recommendation and committed the
immediate implementation of the required adjustments and disclosures.

2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary
adjusting entry to correct the year-end balance of the Cash-in-Bank account
and strictly observe the check disbursement process and reporting to maintain
the reliability of the account presentation.
2.7 The Municipal Accountant assured the Audit Team on the immediate
implementation of the recommendation of drawing the necessary adjusting entry to
correct the Cash account and the strict implementation of the check disbursement
process.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
undertake collaborative procedures to reconcile their respective records based
on available reliable source documents to ascertain the accuracy and fair
presentation of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts.
3.7 During our exit conference, Municipal Treasurer committed to immediately
undertake the necessary collaborative procedures of reconciliation with the
Municipal Accountant to attain accurate and fair presentation of the RPT/SET
Receivable accounts.

4.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant –
a) Immediately draw an adjusting entry for the inclusion of the bank account
with an existing balance to arrive at the correct amount for the Cash-in-
Bank account; and
b) Process the closing of the bank account to recover the existing balance and
avoid the gradual charges or deduction thereof as a dormant account.
4.5 During our exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to the
recommendation and committed the immiedate adjustment of the Cash-in-Bank
account. Also, the Mayor assured the Audit Team on the immediate closure of the
bank account that is no longer use in their operation to recover the existing balance
thereof and avoid the gradual deduction due to dormancy.

5.9 We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Assessor fast-track the updating of the assessment roll through
a frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical
and manpower support are provided to that Office.
b) Municipal Treasurer strategizes and strengthen collection drive beyond
the required posting and sending out of notices to support and sustain the
LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its
constituents.
5.10 The Municipal Assessor reported that updating of assessment roll is on-going but
they need additional IT Personnel for data encoding. The Municipal Mayor
expressed support to the recommendation by giving additional IT personnel to the
Assessor’s Office.
5.11 The Municipal Treasurer was confident on the effectivity of their collection efforts
and assured the Audit Team to do more and give more to achieve higher collections.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly implement the
provisions of the Municipal Ordinance to secure delinquent rentals and
surcharges. Also, consider other proactive strategies to enjoin the stall holders
to pay their delinquencies to sustain the local revenue sources beneficial to the
LGU.
6.8 During our exit conference, the Mayor appreciated the issued AOM to be used as
basis on fully enforcing the Municipal Ordinance. Accordingly, they will meet the
stall holders for more collaborative terms in order to collect the delinquent rental
fees and encourage them to keep-up with their dues.
6.9 The Municipal Treasurer seconded on the comments of the Mayor and assured the
Audit Team of their extended efforts to reach out the stall holders paying their
monthly dues. Also, the Treasurer added that the Market Code was currently
revisited and subjected for review by the sanggunian, thus, it will be more
responsive and relevant to the current rates including the required execution of lease
contract with the stall holders to guarantee prompt payments.

7.8 We recommended that the:
a) Local Chief Executive rescinds the individual contract of service to
consider the daily rate at a reasonable amount instead of monthly
payments, to maintain equality and fairness among personnel hired under
the same contract, and, secure the required authorization from the local
sanggunian before entering into contract of service with private
individuals; and,
b) HRMO strictly observe the conditions set forth under the CSC Resolution
No. 020790 to facilitate an appropriate term with the hired private
individuals through a Job Order Contract or Contract of Services.
7.9 The Mayor agreed to the recommendation of rescinding the existing individual
contracts of services and assured the Audit Team that prior authorization of the
local sanggunian will be secured first before they enter into contract of services on
the succeeding batch of JOs.
7.10 The HRMO committed on the strict compliance of the requirements and
prohibitions laid in CSC Resolution as they enter into contract with private
individuals as JOs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately reverse or restore the unreleased checks that had been outstanding for more than six months to its proper Cash in Bank account, as required in Section 59, Volume I, NGAS for Local.
observations.reason: totaling P 10,235.74 to immediately reverse the Cash in Bank or restore the unreleased checks that had been outstanding for more than six months to its proper Cash in Bank account to establish reliable and properly valued account through an adjusting entry

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal General Services Officer on the immediate accomplishment of the required IIRUP disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the required one-time cleansing of the account and obtain higher miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to fairly state the PPE account and afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-making including the proper establishment of accountability over the assets. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officers/personnel for the missing PPEs
observations.reason: and in relation to the COA disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the required one-time cleansing of the account and obtain higher miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to fairly state the PPE account and afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-making including the proper establishment of accountability over the assets. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officers/personnel for the missing PPEs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting journal entries on the Depreciation account to establish the correct balances of the respective road network components in the financial statements. And also instruct the Municipal Accountant to earnestly comply with the required maintenance of subsidiary records for the roads network components to ascertain the reliability of the PPE account as presented in the financial statements
observations.reason: that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting journal entries on the Depreciation account to establish the correct balances of the respective road network components in the financial statements. And also instruct the Municipal Accountant to earnestly comply with the required maintenance of subsidiary records for the roads network components to ascertain the reliability of the PPE account as presented in the financial statements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MGSO to update the Biological Assets Property Cards and maintain the same appropriately to facilitate compliance with the required disclosures in the financial statements that will establish the reliability of the Biological Asset account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe judicious application of the negotiated procurement through emergency purchase modality only on instances or situations where imminent danger to life or property are present to adhere the economic procurement as declared policy of the State
observations.reason: that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe judicious application of the negotiated procurement through emergency purchase modality only on instances or situations where imminent danger to life or property are present to adhere the economic procurement as declared policy of the State

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensures the prescribed timeline for budget preparation and submission be strictly observed for the on time enactment of the Annual Budget so that implementation of the projects under the mandatory budgetary items such as the LDRRMF, specifically the 70% disaster prevention and mitigation fund, be attained within the local fiscal year that will reduce disaster risks and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the local communities
observations.reason: that the Local Chief Executive ensures the prescribed timeline for budget preparation and submission be strictly observed for the on time enactment of the Annual Budget so that implementation of the projects under the mandatory budgetary items such as the LDRRMF, specifically the 70% disaster prevention and mitigation fund, be attained within the local fiscal year that will reduce disaster risks and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the local communities

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF shall be approved so as to direct the funds for its intended use and afford the community on the benefits of disaster risk prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery
observations.reason: that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF shall be approved so as to direct the funds for its intended use and afford the community on the benefits of disaster risk prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. direct the LDRRM Officer to strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM-related PPAs to achieve the goals and objectives of the fund; b. direct the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to refrain from charging expenses to LDRRMF that are not in accordance with Section 3.0 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and c. require the Mun. Accountant and the MGSO to submit the required documents for further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and to ensure that all the supporting documents are properly signed and provided with complete information.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to immediately reverse or restore the unreleased checks that had
been outstanding for more than six months to its proper Cash in Bank account
to establish reliable and properly valued account through the following adjusting
entry:

(Dr) Cash in Bank – LCCA P 10,235.74
(Cr) Accounts Payable P 10,235.74

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
Municipal General Services Officer on the immediate accomplishment of the
required IIRUP and complete the disposal of those unserviceable properties
in compliance with the one-time cleansing of the account and obtain higher
miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require
the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only
after its disposal to fairly state the PPE account and afford the LGU of reliable
and useful information in decision-making including the proper establishment
of accountability over the assets. Further, require the Municipal Accountant
to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable
officers/personnel for the missing PPEs.

3.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the
Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting journal entries on the
Depreciation account to establish the correct balances of the respective road
network components in the financial statements. And also instruct the
Municipal Accountant to earnestly comply with the required maintenance of
subsidiary records for the roads network components to ascertain the
reliability of the PPE account as presented in the financial statements.

4.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MGSO to update
the Biological Assets Property Cards and maintain the same appropriately to
facilitate compliance with the required disclosures in the financial statements
that will establish the reliability of the Biological Asset account.

5.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal
Planning Development Officer on any pending projects of the LGU funded by
National Government Agencies to ascertain the amount attributable to the
Due to NGAs-Other account and establish the required subsidiary ledgers to
attain reliable and accurate amount of the account, thus, take opportunity on
the benefits from the original projects identified on the fund.

6.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards
Committee to strictly observe judicious application of the negotiated
procurement through emergency purchase modality only on instances or
situations where imminent danger to life or property are present to adhere the
economic procurement as declared policy of the State.

7.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately consult the
Provincial Legal Officer on the initiation of the expropriation proceedings by
filing a verified complaint before the proper court against the property owners
of the site locations for the development projects to promote the general
welfare of the people.

8.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensures the prescribed
timeline for budget preparation and submission be strictly observed for the on
time enactment of the Annual Budget so that implementation of the projects
under the mandatory budgetary items such as the LDRRMF, specifically the
70% disaster prevention and mitigation fund, be attained within the fiscal year
that will reduce disaster risks and enhance disaster preparedness and response
capabilities of the local communities.

9.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly ensure that only
those allowable charges against the LDRRMF shall be approved so as to direct
the funds for its intended use and afford the community on the benefits of
disaster risk prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation
and recovery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in accordance with Accounting Circular No. 2006-001 and COA Circular No. 97-002, otherwise, withhold payment of any money due them in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended that a collection letter be served to the person who is on AWOL/Resigned status.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the proper recognition of the BGCM, adjust the misclassified accounts in the books of the LGU and submit to the Audit Team the distribution lists for the 457 sacks of rice amounting to P1,005,400.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee to submit the required documents for further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to the guidelines of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 01 dated March 27, 2020 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and to ensure that all supporting documents are properly signed and provided with complete information.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GAD focal person to maximize the utilization of GAD funds and to establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 5.1 and 5.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive approve only the valid work suspensions as recommended in Revised IRR of R.A 9184 and direct the municipal officials concerned to consider the unfavorable situations in determining the duration of the projects in order not to hamper the economic benefits it can give to the constituents. Further, we also recommend to require the Municipal Engineer to compute the number of delays in the implementation of projects and apply liquidated damages as penalty to the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
1. direct the Municipal Treasurer to take necessary steps to strengthen the tax
collection strategies of the agency;
2. direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish a precise monitoring of
delinquent tax payers;
3. direct the Municipal Assessor to recommend to the sanggunian the strategies
relating to tax ordinance and revenue measures for the collection of RPT;
and
4. direct the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality to support the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Assessor in strengthening the agency’s collection
strategies.

recommendation and the OIC Municipal Treasurer committed to attain 35% increase in
RPT collections in 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the
accounts and reports to COA within the period prescribed by existing laws and
regulations.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated
under COA Circular No. 92-389, to maintain a sound internal control in the
disbursement of public funds. The perforation or stamping of paid disbursement
vouchers including all its supporting documents is an internal control procedure on
the prevention of presenting the same documents in the future for claims of the
same nature.

Assessor and the Mun. Treasurer in strengthening the LGU’s collection strategies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
 The Mun. Accountant and Mun. Treasurer submit
immediately all the unsubmitted Disbursements Vouchers with the complete
supporting documents to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
 All laws, rules, regulations relative to the submission of accounts
should be strictly followed.

We recommend the LGU to require the Accountable Officers (AOs) to
prepare the Monthly Report of Collections and Deposits as required by the
NGAS Manual, P.D. 1445 and other related IRR to facilitate the monitoring
and analysis of cash movement, strengthen control over its collections and to
have accurate and adequate information for better planning and decision-
making.

We recommend that:
 Collections be immediately remitted at the closing of the day or
the day immediately following the date of collection so as to avoid
potential loss and/or inappropriate usage of government funds;
 All collections and remittances be properly recorded in the books and
promptly deposited to the bank within the period legally stated under
related IRR.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
reconcile their respective balances and to record the necessary adjustments
to cure the discrepancies in their records.

We recommend management to instruct the accountable officers to strictly
adhere to provision of Section 73 of the GAAM, Volume I and issue official
receipts while following correct and appropriate numerical sequence so as to
avoid suspicions of potential malversation.

We recommend that:
 The BAC verify, validate and ascertain that all statements made in the
contract and documents submitted with it adheres to the guidelines in
the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act
9184.
 The Head of Procuring Entity enters into contract with the
winning bidder only if all the documentary requirements are
complied with.

We recommend management to comply strictly with Section 52 of RA
No. 10717 or the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2016
and that they would not act on additional loan applications unless the
P3,500.00 net take home pay is satisfied.

We recommend management to:
 Require the employees concerned to submit duly accomplished Driver’s
Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travels, Requisition and Issue
Voucher and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, in compliance with
COA Circular No. 77-61 and on the prescribed Manual on Audit of Fuel
Consumption of Government Vehicles.
 Submit procurement documents with correct and complete
documentation as provided in Section 4 of P.D. 1445.

We recommend that the Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan to submit written justifications with supporting documents why the Budget for Calendar Year 2015 was not approved.
observations.reason: The documents why the Budget for Calendar Year 2015 was not approved were submitted.

observations.recommendation: Management should: 1. Serve demand letters to those officials and employees, requiring them to settle or liquidate immediately their advances otherwise, suspend their salaries until full liquidation has been made; 2. Stop the practice of granting cash advances for travels of officials and employees with outstanding and unliquidated cash advances; and 3. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger cards for each official/employee who are granted cash advances for monitoring purposes.

Conduct fair canvass for the procurement of supplies, materials and equipment within the locality to avail of a more competitive price, avoid additional or unnecessary expenditures relating thereto, and the scarce resources of the government could be fully utilized on projects beneficial to the public as a whole.
observations.reason: Management continuously improves their procurement strategy to meet or surpass collection targets; however full participation of taxpayers in programs is still being encouraged.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC Accountant to submit immediately the lacking documents to the audit team. Henceforth, management should see to it that the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and relevant provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2014 and RA 9184 be strictly followed/complied. Likewise, refrain from processing claims which are not supported with complete documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Management submitted the required documents to the audit team. Other documents are still pending submission.

observations.recommendation: Adopt more effective collection strategies and intensify collection campaign to meet or surpass collection targets for the year.
observations.reason: Management continuously improves collection strategy to meet targets; however participation of taxpayers remain variable.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality shall strictly observe the limitations on the grant of PEI set forth in DBM Budget Circular No. 2011-4 and exercise prudence in salary allocations for personal services, subject to the exceptions identified in DBM Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 75 dated July 12, 2002 and subsequent pertinent laws and regulations.
observations.reason: Management will exercise prudence and adhere to DBM circulars; some adjustments remain subject to applicable exceptions and approvals.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a survey of the land and land improvements be conducted as a requisite for the preparation of the technical description of the properties and preparatory to the issuance of certificate of title or Torrens System registration through Free Patent or Cadastral System.
observations.managementAction: AAR, for Land Title and Registratio n
observations.reason: Application for land title and registration is still pending. Management promised to follow-up the same.
